LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KERRI NAU'S MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE ITS PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. COURTS SHOULD EXERCISE THEIR DISCRETION TO ALLOW AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS "LIBERALLY IN FAVOR OF AMENDMENTS." (KITTREDGE SPORTS CO. V. SUP. CT. (1989) 213 CAL.APP.3D 1045, 1047.) THE COURT DECLINES TO RULE ON THE OBJECTIONS TO THE CONTENT OF THE PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AT THIS TIME. IF DEFENDANT WISHES TO CHALLENGE THE PLEADINGS, HE MAY FILE A SEPARATE MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-22-2015 Continued To May-26-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-26-2015 Dismissal - No Appearance

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Set For Trial On May-26-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kathleen Duggan, Esq. of Roper Majeski, for defendant and moving party;
Leslie Mitchell, Esq. Piering Law Firm, Tel. 916.520.5255, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CARLOS RAMOS' MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Conversion can support plaintiff's claim for punitive damages. See Ferraro v. Pacific Fin. Corp. (1970) 8 Cal.App.3d 339, 351 ("Exemplary damages are properly awardable in an action for conversion, given the required showing of malice, fraud, or oppression.") (citation omitted). Paragraphs 58, 60, 63, 64 of the conversion cause of action allege that Ramos "grabbed" Nau's cell phone with the intent to keep and convert it to his own use and possession. Paragraphs 59, 63, 64 allege that Ramos took Nau's wallet and converted it to his own possession and use. These allegations of an intentional tort are taken as true and support the claim for punitives. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MINI MINUTES:

Kathleen Duggan, Esq. of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley, Tel. 213.312.2072, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: A R G U E D. After argument, the Court vacated the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CARLOS RAMOS' MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is granted. Motion to strike the claim for attorney fees is granted as there is no contractual or statutory basis for this claim. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF KERRI NAU'S Motion To Amend Complaint is granted. Plaintiff may file its proposed First Amended Complaint within 10 days entry of this order. Courts should exercise their discretion to allow amendments to pleadings "liberally in favor of amendments." (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1047.) The Court declines to rule on the objections to the content of the proposed First Amended Complaint at this time. If Defendant wishes to challenge the pleadings, he may file a separate motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
